description:
## Question

How must the backend, RPC boundary, and Issue Explorer coordinate a switch so a target Workspace is validated and loaded through Beadwork before it becomes Current, while the old workspace remains authoritative on failures? Resolve path threading, startup restoration, concurrent/in-flight load safety, error/banner semantics, and the boundary between persisted known and active Workspaces.

comments.text: HITL decision: latest selection wins. When a new Workspace is chosen while another candidate is validating or loading, it replaces the pending target immediately. The prior attempt may finish, but its result is discarded by request generation; the old Current Workspace remains authoritative until the newest candidate commits.

HITL decision: startup restoration is provisional. A remembered last Workspace becomes Current Workspace only after validation and a complete Issue Explorer load succeed. If loading fails, retain it as a retryable remembered Workspace and leave the app with no Current Workspace rather than presenting a failed workspace as Current.

## Resolution

The complete transaction specification is in [atomic-workspace-switch-transaction.md](docs/research/atomic-workspace-switch-transaction.md).

The backend owns catalog, Pending Workspace, and Current Workspace; issue-loading RPCs stay path-free and every bw subprocess receives an explicit backend-selected cwd. A candidate is known after validation but becomes Current only after all Issue Explorer views load and the Current/MRU store update saves. The old snapshot remains visible throughout a switch and every failure.

HITL decisions captured: latest selection wins via generation checks; startup restoration is provisional and a failed load leaves no Current Workspace; persistence failure prevents the visual switch.

title: Specify the atomic workspace-switch transaction
